Figure 1:Examples of the utility matrices for Prisoner’s Dilemma (upper left) and Stag Hunt (lower left). In each cell, the number on the left (right) is the utility for the row (column) player.
The figure on the right plots the average utility across10,00010,000runs of the experiments when both players in Stag-Hunt (lower left) are minimizingLRP-Regretfor100,000100,000iterations. In each run of the experiment, the initial strategy is randomly sampled uniformly. We choose the learning rateη=0.01\eta=0.01in4.3with different memory lengthsM=1,2,3M=1,2,3. As shown in the figure,LRP-Regretminimization can encourage players to converge to abetter equilibriumin terms of utility.
